author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

ccel.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

title: Psalm LI

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Neither must this multitude’s throng be defrauded, nor their infirmity burthened.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Silence we ask, and quiet, in order that our voice, after yesterday’s labour, be able with some little vigour to last out.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It must be believed, that your love hath met together in greater numbers to-day for nothing else, but that ye may pray for those whom an alien and perverse inclination doth keep away.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For we are speaking neither of heathens nor of Jews, but of Christians: nor of those that are yet Catechumens, but of many that are even baptized, from the Laver of whom ye do no wise differ, and yet to their heart ye are unlike.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For to-day how many brethren of ours we think of, and deplore their going unto vanities and lying insanities, to the neglect of that to which they have been called.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who, if in the very circus from any cause they chance to be startled, do immediately cross themselves, and stand bearing It on the forehead, in the very place, from whence they had withdrawn, if they had borne It in heart.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: God’s mercy must be implored, that He may give understanding for condemning these things, inclination to flee them, and mercy to forgive.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Opportunately, then, of Penitence a Psalm to-day has been chanted.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Speak we even with the absent: there will be to them for our voice your memory.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Neglect not the wounded and feeble, but that ye may more easily make whole, whole ye ought to abide.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Correct by reproving, comfort by addressing, set an example by living well, He will be with them that hath been with you.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For now that ye have overpassed these dangers, the fountain of God’s mercy is not closed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Where ye have come they will come; where ye have passed they will pass.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: A grievous thing it is indeed, and exceeding perilous, nay ruinous, and for certain a deadly thing, that witting they sin.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For in one way to these vanities doth he run that despiseth the voice of Christ; in another way, he that knoweth from what he is fleeing.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But that not even of such men we ought to despair, this Psalm doth show.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For there is written over it the title thereof, “A Psalm of David himself, when there came to him Nathan the prophet, when he went in unto Bersabee.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Bersabee was a woman, wife of another.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: With grief indeed we speak, and with trembling; but yet God would not have to be hushed what He hath willed to be written.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I will say then not what I will, but what I am obliged; I will say not as one exhorting to imitation, but as one instructing you to fear.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Captivated with this woman’s beauty, the wife of another, the king and prophet David, from whose seed according to the flesh the Lord was to come, committed adultery with her.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This thing in this Psalm is not read, but in the title thereof it appeareth; but in the book of Kings it is more fully read.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Both Scriptures are canonical, to both without any doubt by Christians credit must be given.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The sin was committed, and was written down.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Moreover her husband in war he caused to be killed: and after this deed there was sent to him Nathan the prophet; sent by the Lord, to reprove him for so great an outrage.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What men should beware of, we have said; but what if they shall have fallen they should imitate, let us hear.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For many men will to fall with David, and will not to rise with David.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not then for falling is the example set forth, but if thou shalt have fallen for rising again.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Take heed lest thou fall.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not the delight of the younger be the lapse of the elder, but be the fall of the elder the dread of the younger.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For this it was set forth, for this was written, for this in the Church often read and chanted: let them hear that have not fallen, lest they fall; let them hear that have fallen, that they may rise.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: So great a man’s sin is not hushed, is proclaimed in the Church.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There men hear that are ill hearers, and seek for themselves countenance for sinning: they look out for means whereby they may defend what they have made ready to commit, not how they may beware of what they have not committed, and they say to themselves, If David, why not I too?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thence that soul is more unrighteous, which, forasmuch as it hath done it because David did, therefore hath done worse than David.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I will say this very thing, if I shall be able, more plainly.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: David had set forth to himself none for a precedent as thou hast: he had fallen by lapse of concupiscence, not by the countenance of holiness: thou dost set before thine eyes as it were a holy man, in order that thou mayest sin: thou dost not copy his holiness, but dost copy his fall.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou dost love that in David, which in himself David hated: thou makest thee ready to sin, thou inclinest to sin: in order that thou mayest sin thou consultest the book of God: the Scriptures of God for this thou hearest, that thou mayest do what displeaseth God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This did not David; he was reproved by a Prophet, he stumbled not over a Prophet.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But others hearing to their health, by the fall of a strong man measure their weakness: and desiring to avoid what God condemneth, from careless looking do restrain their eyes.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Them they fix not upon the beauty of another’s flesh, nor make themselves careless with perverse simpleness; they say not, “With good intent I have observed, of kindness I have observed, of charity I have long looked.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For they set before themselves the fall of David, and they see that this great man for this purpose hath fallen, in order that little men may not be willing to look on that whereby they may fall.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For they restrain their eyes from wantonness, not readily do they join themselves in company, they do not mingle with strange women, they raise not complying eyes to strange balconies, to strange terraces.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For from afar David saw her with whom he was captivated.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Woman afar, lust near.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What he saw was elsewhere, in himself that whereby he fell.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This weakness of the flesh must be therefore minded, the words of the Apostle recollected, “Let not sin therefore reign in your mortal body.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He hath not said, let there not be; but, “let there not reign.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There is sin in thee, when thou takest pleasure; there reigneth, if thou shalt have consented.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Carnal pleasure, especially if proceeding unto unlawful and strange objects, is to be bridled, not let loose: by government to be tamed, not to be set up for government.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Look and be without care, if thou hast nothing whereby thou mayest be moved.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But thou makest answer, “I contain with strong resolution.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Art thou any wise stronger than David?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He admonisheth, moreover, by such an example, that no one ought to lift himself up in prosperous circumstances.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For many fear adverse circumstances, fear not prosperous circumstances.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Prosperity is more perilous to soul than adversity to body.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: First, prosperity doth corrupt, in order that adversity may find something to break.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: My brethren, stricter watch must be kept against felicity.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wherefore, see ye after what manner the saying of God amid our own felicity doth take from us security: “Serve ye,” He saith, “the Lord in fear, and exult unto Him with trembling.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In exultation, in order that we may render thanks; in trembling, lest we fall.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This sin did not David, when he was suffering Saul for persecutor.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When holy David was suffering Saul his enemy, when he was being vexed by his persecutions, when he was fleeing through divers places, in order that he might not fall into his hands, he lusted not for her that was another’s, he slew not husband after committing adultery with wife.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He was in the infirmity of his tribulation so much the more intimate with God as he seemed more miserable.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Something useful is tribulation; useful the surgeon’s lancet rather than the devil’s temptation.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He became secure when his enemies were overthrown, pressure was removed, swelling grew out.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This example therefore doth avail to this end, that we should fear felicity.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Tribulation,” he saith, “and grief I found, and on the name of the Lord I called.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But it was done; I would say these words to those that have not done the like, in order that they should watch to keep their uncorruptness, and that while they take heed how a great one has fallen, they that be small should fear.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But if any that hath already fallen heareth these words, and that hath in his conscience any evil thing; to the words of this Psalm let him advert; let him heed the greatness of the wound, but not despair of the majesty of the Physician.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Sin with despair is certain death.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let no one therefore say, If already any evil thing I have done, already I am to be condemned: God pardoneth not such evil things, why add I not sins to sins?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I will enjoy this word in pleasure, in wantonness, in wicked cupidity: now hope of amendment having been lost, let me have even what I see, if I cannot have what I believe.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This Psalm then, while it maketh heedful those that have not believed, so doth not will them that have fallen to be despaired of.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whoever thou art that hast sinned, and hesitatest to exercise penitence for thy sin, despairing of thy salvation, hear David groaning.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To thee Nathan the prophet hath not been sent, David himself hath been sent to thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hear him crying, and with him cry: hear him groaning, and with him groan; hear him weeping, and mingle tears; hear him amended, and with him rejoice.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If from thee sin could not be excluded, be not hope of pardon excluded.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There was sent to that man Nathan the prophet, observe the king’s humility.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He rejected not the words of him giving admonition, he said not, Darest thou speak to me, a king?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: An exalted king heard a prophet, let His humble people hear Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hear therefore these words, and say thou with him: “Have pity upon me, O God, after Thy great mercy” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He that imploreth great mercy, confesseth great misery.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let them seek a little mercy of Thee, that have sinned in ignorance: “Have pity,” he saith, “upon me, after Thy great mercy.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Relieve a deep wound after Thy great healing.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Deep is what I have, but in the Almighty I take refuge.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Of my own so deadly wound I should despair, unless I could find so great a Physician.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Have pity upon me, O God, after Thy great mercy: and after the multitude of Thy pities, blot out my iniquity.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What he saith, “Blot out my iniquity,” is this, “Have pity upon me, O God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And what he saith, “After the multitude of Thy pities,” is this, “After Thy great mercy.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because great is the mercy, many are the mercies; and of Thy great mercy, many are Thy pitying.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou dost regard mockers to amend them, dost regard ignorant men to teach them, dost regard men confessing to pardon.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Did he this in ignorance?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: A certain man had done some, aye many evil things he had done; “Mercy,” he saith, “I obtained, because ignorant I did it in unbelief.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This David could not say, “Ignorant I did it.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For he was not ignorant how very evil a thing was the touching of another’s wife, and how very evil a thing was the killing of the husband, who knew not of it, and was not even angered.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They obtain therefore the mercy of the Lord that have in ignorance done it; and they that have knowing done it, obtain not any mercy it may chance, but “great mercy.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “More and more wash me from mine unrighteousness” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is, “More and more wash”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: One much stained.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: More and more wash the sins of one knowing.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou that hast washed off the sins of one ignorant.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not even thus is it to be despaired of Thy mercy.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And from my delinquency purge Thou me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: According to the manner in which He is physician, offer a recompense.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He is God, offer sacrifice.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What wilt thou give that thou mayest be purged?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For see upon whom thou callest; upon a Just One thou callest.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He hateth sins, if He is just; He taketh vengeance upon sins, if He is just; thou wilt not be able to take away from the Lord God His justice: entreat mercy, but observe the justice: there is mercy to pardon the sinner, there is justice to punish the sin.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou askest mercy; shall sin unpunished abide?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let David answer, let those that have fallen answer, answer with David, and say, No, Lord, no sin of mine shall be unpunished; I know the justice of Him whose mercy I ask: it shall not be unpunished, but for this reason I will not that Thou punish me, because I punish my sin: for this reason I beg that Thou pardon, because I acknowledge.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For mine iniquity I acknowledge, and my delinquency is before me ever” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I have not put behind my back what I have done, I look not at others, forgetful of myself, I pretend not to pull out a straw from my brother’s eye, when there is a beam in my eye; my sin is before me, not behind me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For it was behind me when to me was sent the Prophet, and set before me the parable of the poor man’s sheep.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For saith Nathan the Prophet to David, “There was a certain rich man having very many sheep; but a poor man his neighbour had one little ewe sheep, which in his bosom and of his own food he was feeding: there came a stranger to the rich man, nothing from his flock he took, for the lithe ewe sheep of the poor man his neighbour he lusted; her he slew for the stranger: what doth he deserve?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But the other being angry doth pronounce sentence: then the king, evidently knowing not wherein he had been taken, declared the rich man deserving of death, and that the sheep be restored fourfold.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Most sternly and most justly.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But his sin was not yet before him, behind his back was what he had done: his own iniquity he did not yet acknowledge, and therefore another’s he did not pardon.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But the Prophet, being for this purpose sent, took from his back the sin, and before his eyes placed it, so that he might see that sentence so stern to have been pronounced against himself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For cutting and healing his heart’s wound, he made a lancet of his tongue.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Against Thee alone have I sinned, and before Thee an evil thing have I done” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is this?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For before men was not another’s wife debauched and husband slain?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Did not all men know what David had done?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is, “Against Thee alone have I sinned, and before Thee an evil thing have I done.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because Thou alone art without sin.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He is a just punisher that hath nothing in Him to be punished; He is a just reprover that hath nothing in Him to be reproved.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “That thou mayest be justified in Thy sayings, and conquer when Thou art judged.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To whom he speaketh, brethren, to whom he speaketh, is difficult to understand.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To God surely he speaketh, and it is evident that God the Father is not judged.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is, “And conquer when Thou art judged”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He seeth the future Judge to be judged, one just by sinners to be judged, and therein conquering, because in Him was nothing to be judged.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For alone among men could truly say the God-Man, “If ye have found in Me sin, say.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But perchance there was what escaped men, and they found not what was really there, but was not manifest.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In another place He saith, “Behold there cometh the Prince of the world,” being an acute observer of all sins; “Behold,” He saith, “there cometh the Prince of this world,” with death afflicting sinners, presiding over death: for, “By the malice of the devil death came into the world.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Behold,” He saith, “there cometh the Prince of the world:”—He said these words close upon His Passion:—“and in Me he shall find nothing,” nothing of sin, nothing worthy of death, nothing worthy of condemnation.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And as if it were said to Him, Why then dost Thou die?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He continueth and saith, “But that all men may know that I do the will of My Father; arise, let us go hence.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I suffer, He saith, undeserving, for men deserving, in order that them I may make deserving of My Life, for whom I undeservedly suffer their death.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To Him then, having no sin, saith on the present occasion the Prophet David, “Against Thee only have I sinned, and before Thee an evil thing have I done, that Thou mayest be justified in Thy sayings, and conquer when Thou art judged.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For Thou overcomest all men, all judges; and he that deemeth himself just, before Thee is unjust: Thou alone justly judgest, having been unjustly judged, That hast power to lay down Thy life, and hast power again to take it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou conquerest, then, when Thou art judged.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: All men Thou overcomest, because Thou art more than men, and by Thee were men made.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For, behold, in iniquities I was conceived” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: As though he were saying, They are conquered that have done what thou, David, hast done: for this is not a little evil and little sin, to wit, adultery and man-slaying.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What of them that from the day that they were born of their mother’s womb, have done no such thing? even to them dost thou ascribe some sins, in order that He may conquer all men when He beginneth to be judged.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: David hath taken upon him the person of mankind, and hath heeded the bonds of all men, hath considered the offspring of death, hath adverted to the origin of iniquity, and he saith, “For, behold, in iniquities I was conceived.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Was David born of adultery; being born of Jesse, a righteous man, and his own wife?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is it that he saith himself to have been in iniquity conceived, except that iniquity is drawn from Adam?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Even the very bond of death, with iniquity itself is engrained?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: No man is born without bringing punishment, bringing desert of punishment.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: A Prophet saith also in another place, “No one is clean in Thy sight, not even an infant, whose life is of one day upon earth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For we know both by the Baptism of Christ that sins are loosed, and that the Baptism of Christ availeth the remission of sins.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If infants are every way innocent, why do mothers run with them when sick to the Church?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What by that Baptism, what by that remission is put away?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: An innocent one I see that rather weeps than is angry.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What doth Baptism wash off? what doth that Grace loose?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There is loosed the offspring of sin.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For if that infant could speak to thee, it would say, and if it had the understanding which David had, it would answer thee, Why heedest thou me, an infant?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou dost not indeed see my actions: but I in iniquity have been conceived, “And in sins hath my mother nourished me in the womb.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Apart from this bond of mortal concupiscence was Christ born without a male, of a virgin conceiving by the Holy Ghost.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He cannot be said to have been conceived in iniquity, it cannot be said, In sins His mother nourished Him in the womb, to whom was said, “The Holy Ghost shall come upon thee, and the Virtue of the Highest shall overshadow thee.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is not therefore because it is sin to have to do with wives that men are conceived in iniquity, and in sins nourished in the womb by their mother; but because that which is made is surely made of flesh deserving punishment.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the punishment of the flesh is death, and surely there is in it liability to death itself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whence the Apostle spoke not of the body as if to die, but as if dead: “The body indeed is dead,” he saith, “because of sin, but the Spirit is life because of righteousness.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How then without bond of sin is born that which is conceived and sown of a body dead because of sin?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This chaste operation in a married person hath not sin, but the origin of sin draweth with it condign punishment.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For there is no husband that, because he is an husband, is not subject to death, or that is subject to death for any other reason but because of sin.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For even the Lord was subject to death, but not on account of sin: He took upon Him our punishment, and so looseth our guilt.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: With reason then, “In Adam all die, but in Christ shall all be made alive.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For, “Through one man,” saith the Apostle, “sin hath entered into this world, and through sin death, and so hath passed unto all men, in that all have sinned.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Definite is the sentence: “In Adam,” he saith, “all have sinned.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Alone then could such an infant be innocent, as hath not been born of the work of Adam.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For, behold, truth Thou hast loved: uncertain and hidden things of Thy wisdom, Thou hast manifested to me” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That is, Thou hast not left unpunished even the sins of those whom Thou dost pardon.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Truth Thou hast loved:” so mercy Thou hast granted first, as that Thou shouldest also preserve truth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou pardonest one confessing, pardonest, but only if he punisheth himself: so there are preserved mercy and truth: mercy because man is set free; truth, because sin is punished.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Uncertain and hidden things of Thy wisdom Thou hast manifested to me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What “hidden things”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What “uncertain things”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because God pardoneth even such.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Nothing is so hidden, nothing so uncertain.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For this uncertainty the Ninevites repented, for they said, though after the threatenings of the Prophet, though after that cry, “Three days and Nineve shall be overthrown:” they said to themselves, Mercy must be implored; they said in this sort reasoning among themselves, “Who knoweth whether God may turn for the better His sentence, and have pity?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It was “uncertain,” when it is said, “Who knoweth?” on an uncertainty they did repent, certain mercy they earned: they prostrated them in tears, in fastings, in sackcloth and ashes they prostrated them, groaned, wept, God spared.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Nineve stood: was Nineve overthrown?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: One way indeed it seemeth to men, and another way it seemed to God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But I think that it was fulfilled that the Prophet had foretold.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Regard what Nineve was, and see how it was overthrown; overthrown in evil, builded in good; just as Saul the persecutor was overthrown, Paul the preacher builded.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who would not say that this city, in which we now are, was happily overthrown, if all those madmen, leaving their triflings, were to run together to the Church with contrite heart, and were to call upon God’s mercy for their past doings?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Should we not say, Where is that Carthage?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because there is not what there was, it is overthrown: but if there is what there was not, it is builded.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: So is said to Jeremiah, “Behold, I will give to thee to root up, to dig under, to overthrow, to destroy,” and again, “to build, and to plant.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thence is that voice of the Lord, “I will smite and I will heal.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He smiteth the rottenness of the deed, He healeth the pain of the wound.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Physicians do thus when they cut; they smite and heal; they arm themselves in order to strike, they carry steel, and come to cure.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But because great were the sins of the Ninevites, they said, “Who knoweth?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This uncertainty had God disclosed to His servant David.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For when he had said, before the Prophet standing and convicting him, “I have sinned:” straightway he heard from the Prophet, that is, from the Spirit of God which was in the Prophet, “Thy sin is put away from thee.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Uncertain and hidden things” of His wisdom He manifested to him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou shalt sprinkle me,” he saith, “with hyssop, and I shall be cleansed” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hyssop we know to be a herb humble but healing: to the rock it is said to adhere with roots.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thence in a mystery the similitude of cleansing the heart has been taken.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Do thou also take hold, with the root of thy love, on thy Rock: be humble in thy humble God, in order that thou mayest be exalted in thy glorified God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou shalt be sprinkled with hyssop, the humility of Christ shall cleanse thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Despise not the herb, attend to the efficacy of the medicine.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Something further I will say, which we are wont to hear from physicians, or to experience in sick persons.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hyssop, they say, is proper for purging the lungs.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In the lung is wont to be noted pride: for there is inflation, there breathing.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It was said of Saul the persecutor as of Saul the proud, that he was going to bind Christians, breathing slaughter: he was breathing out slaughter, breathing out blood, his lung not yet cleansed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hear also in this place one humbled, because with hyssop purged: “Thou shalt wash me,” that is, shalt cleanse me: “and above snow I shall be whitened.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Although,” he saith, “your sins shall have been like scarlet, like snow I will whiten.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Out of such men Christ doth present to Himself a vesture without spot and wrinkle.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Further, His vesture on the mount, which shone forth like whitened snow, signified the Church cleansed from every spot of sin.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But where is humility from hyssop?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hear what followeth: “To my hearing Thou shall give exultation and gladness, and bones humbled shall exult” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I will rejoice in hearing Thee, not in speaking against Thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou hast sinned, why defendest thou thyself?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou wilt speak: suffer thou; hear, yield to divine words, lest thou be put to confusion, and be still more wounded: sin hath been committed, be it not defended: to confession let it come, not to defence.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou engagest thyself as defender of thy sin, thou art conquered: no innocent patron hast thou engaged, thy defence is not profitable to thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For who art thou that defendest thyself?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou art meet to accuse thyself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Say not, either, “I have done nothing;” or, “What great thing have I done?” or, “Other men as well have done.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If in doing sin thou sayest thou hast done nothing, thou wilt be nothing, thou wilt receive nothing: God is ready to give indulgence, thou closest the door against thyself: He is ready to give, do not oppose the bar of defence, but open the bosom of confession.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “To my hearing Thou shalt give exultation and gladness.”…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Turn Thou away Thy face from my sins, and all mine iniquities blot out” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For now bones humbled exult, now with hyssop cleansed, humble I have become.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Turn Thou away Thy face,” not from me, but “from my sins.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For in another place praying he saith, “Turn not away Thy face from me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He that would not that God’s face be turned away from himself, would that God’s face be turned away from his sins.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For to sin, when God turneth not Himself away, he adverteth: if he adverteth, he animadverteth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And all mine iniquities blot out.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He is busied with that capital sin: he reckoneth on more, he would have all his iniquities to be blotted out: he relieth on the Physician’s hand, on that “great mercy,” upon which he hath called in the beginning of the Psalm: “All mine iniquities blot out.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: God turneth away His face, and so blotteth out; by “turning away” His face, sins He blotteth out.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: By “turning towards,” He writeth them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou hast heard of Him blotting out by turning away, hear of Him by turning towards, doing what?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “But the countenance of the Lord is upon men doing evil things, that He may destroy from the earth the remembrance of them:” He shall destroy the remembrance of them, not by “blotting out their sins.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But here he doth ask what?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Turn away Thy face from my sins.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Well he asketh.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For he himself doth not turn away his face from his own sins, saying, “For my sin I acknowledge.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: With reason thou askest and well askest, that God turn away from thy sin, if thou from thence dost not turn away thy face: but if thou settest thy sin at thy back, God doth there set His face.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Do thou turn sin before thy face, if thou wilt that God thence turn away His face; and then safely thou askest, and He heareth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “A clean heart create in me, O God” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Create”—he meant to say, “as it were begin something new.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But, because repentant he was praying (that had committed some sin, which before he had committed, he was more innocent), after what manner he hath said “create” he showeth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And a right spirit renew in my inner parts.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: By my doing, he saith, the uprightness of my spirit hath been made old and bowed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For he saith in another Psalm, “They have bowed my soul.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And when a man doth make himself stoop unto earthly lusts, he is “bowed” in a manner, but when he is made erect for things above, upright is his heart made, in order that God may be good to him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For, “How good is the God of Israel to the upright of heart!”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Moreover, brethren, listen.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Sometimes God in this world chastiseth for his sin him that He pardoneth in the world to come.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For even to David himself, to whom it had been already said by the Prophet, “Thy sin is put away,” there happened certain things which God had threatened for that very sin.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For his son Abessalom against him waged bloody war, and many ways humbled his father.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He was walking in grief, in the tribulation of his humiliation, so resigned to God, that, ascribing to Him all that was just, he confessed that he was suffering nothing undeservedly, having now an heart upright, to which God was not displeasing.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: A slanderous person and one throwing in his teeth harsh curses he patiently heard, one of the soldiers on the opposite side, that were with his unnatural son.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And when he was heaping curses upon the king, one of the companions of David, enraged, would have gone and smitten him; but he is kept back by David.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And he is kept back how?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For that he said, God sent him to curse me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Acknowledging his guilt he embraced his penance, seeking glory not his own, praising the Lord in that good which he had, praising the Lord in that which he was suffering, “blessing the Lord alway, ever His praise was in his mouth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Such are all the upright in heart: not those crooked persons who think themselves upright and God crooked: who when they do any evil thing, rejoice; when they suffer any evil thing, blaspheme; nay, if set in tribulation and scourging, they say from their distorted heart, “O God, what have I done to Thee?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Truly it is because they have done nothing to God, for they have done all to themselves.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And an upright spirit, renew in my inner parts.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Cast me not forth from Thy face” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Turn away Thy face from my sins: and “cast me not forth from Thy face.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whose face he feareth, upon the face of the Same he calleth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And Thy Holy Spirit take not away from me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For in one confessing there is the Holy Spirit.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Even now, to the gift of the Holy Spirit it belongeth, that what thou hast done displeaseth thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The unclean spirit sins do please; the Holy One they displease.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Though then thou still implore pardon, yet thou art joined to God on the other part, because the evil thing that thou hast committed displeaseth thee: for the same thing displeaseth both thee and Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now, to assail thy fever, ye are two, thou and the Physician.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the reason that there cannot be confession of sin and punishment of sin in a man of himself: when one is angry with himself, and is displeasing to himself, then it is not without the gift of the Holy Spirit, nor doth he say, Thy Holy Spirit give to me, but, “Take not away from me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Give back to me the exultation of Thy salvation” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Give back” what I had; what by sinning I had lost: to wit, of Thy Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For who without Him can be made whole?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because even before that He was Son of Mary, “In the beginning He was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God;” and so, by the holy fathers a future dispensation of flesh taken upon Him, was looked for; as is believed by us to have been done.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Times are changed, not faith.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And with Principal Spirit confirm me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Some have here understood the Trinity in God, Itself God; the dispensation of Flesh being excepted therefrom: since it is written, “God is a Spirit.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For that which is not body, and yet is, seemeth to exist in such sort as that it is spirit.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore some understand here the Trinity spoken of: “In upright Spirit,” the Son; in “Holy Spirit,” Holy Ghost; in “Principal Spirit,” Father.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is not any heretical opinion, therefore, whether this be so, or whether “upright Spirit” He would have to be taken of man himself (when He saith, “An upright spirit renew in my inner parts”), which I have bowed and distorted by sinning, so that in that case the Holy Spirit be Himself the Principal Spirit: which also he would not have to be taken away from him, and thereby would have himself to be confirmed therein.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But see what he annexeth: “With Principal Spirit,” he saith, “confirm Thou me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wherein “confirm”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because Thou hast pardoned me, because I am secure, that what Thou hast forgiven is not to be ascribed, on this being made secure and with this grace confirmed, therefore I am not ungrateful.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But I shall do what?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I would teach unrighteous men Thy ways” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Being myself of the unrighteous (that is, one that was myself an unrighteous man, now no longer unrighteous; the Holy Spirit not having been taken away from me, and I being confirmed with Principal Spirit).

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I would teach unrighteous men Thy ways.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What ways wilt thou teach unrighteous men?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And ungodly men to Thee shall be converted.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If David’s sin is counted for ungodliness, let not ungodly men despair of themselves, forasmuch as God hath spared an ungodly man; but let them take heed that to Him they be converted, that His ways they learn.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But if David’s deed is not counted for ungodliness, but this is properly call ungodliness, namely, to apostatize from God, not to worship one God, or never to have worshipped, or to have forsaken, Him whom one did worship, then what he saith hath the force of superabundance, “And ungodly men shall to Thee be converted.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: So full art thou of the fatness of mercy, that for those converted to Thee, not only sinners of any sort, but even ungodly, there is no cause for despair.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That believing on Him that justifieth an ungodly man, their faith may be counted for righteousness.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Deliver me from bloods, O God, God of my health” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Latin translator hath expressed, though by a word not Latin, yet an accuracy from the Greek.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For we all know that in Latin, sanguines (bloods) are not spoken of, nor yet sanguina (bloods in the neuter), nevertheless because the Greek translator hath thus used the plural number, not without reason, but because he found this in the original language the Hebrew, a godly translator hath preferred to use a word not Latin, rather than one not exact.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wherefore then hath he said in the plural number, “From bloods”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In many bloods, as in the origin of the sinful flesh, many sins he would have to be understood.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Apostle having regard to the very sins which come of the corruption of flesh and blood, saith, “Flesh and blood shall not possess the kingdom of God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For doubtless, after the true faith of the same Apostle, that flesh shall rise again and shall itself gain incorruption, as He saith Himself, “This corruptible must put on incorruption, and this mortal put on immortality.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because then this corruption is of sin, by the name thereof sins are called.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In like manner as both that morsel of flesh and member which playeth in the mouth when we articulate words is called a tongue, and that is called a tongue which by the tongue is made, so we call one tongue the Greek, another the Latin; for the flesh is not diverse, but the sound.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In the same manner, then, as the speech which is made by the tongue is called a tongue; so also the iniquity which is made by blood is called blood.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Heeding, then, his many iniquities, as in the expression above, “And all my iniquities blot out,” and ascribing them to the corruption of flesh and blood, “Free me,” he saith, “from bloods:” that is, free me from iniquities, cleanse me from all corruption.…Not yet is the substance, but certain hope.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And my tongue shall exult of Thy righteousness.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “O Lord, my lips Thou shalt open, and my mouth shall tell of Thy praise” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thy praise,” because I have been created: “Thy praise,” because sinning I have not been forsaken: “Thy praise,” because I have been admonished to confess: “Thy praise,” because in order that I might be secured I have been cleansed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Because if Thou hadst willed sacrifice, I would have given it surely” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: David was living at that time when sacrifices of victim animals were offered to God, and he saw these times that were to be.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Do we not perceive ourselves in these words?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Those sacrifices were figurative, foretelling the One Saving Sacrifice.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not even we have been left without a Sacrifice to offer to God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For hear what he saith, having a concern for his sin, and wishing the evil thing which he hath done to be forgiven him: “If Thou hadst willed,” he saith, “sacrifice, I would have given it surely.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: With holocausts Thou wilt not be delighted.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Nothing shall we therefore offer?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: So shall we come to God?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And whence shall we propitiate Him?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Offer; certainly in thyself thou hast what thou mayest offer.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Do not from without fetch frankincense, but say, “In me are, O God, Thy vows, which I will render of praise to Thee.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Do not from without seek cattle to slay, thou hast in thyself what thou mayest kill.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Sacrifice to God is a spirit troubled, a heart contrite and humbled God despiseth not” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Utterly he despiseth bull, he-goat, ram: now is not the time that these should be offered.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They were offered when they indicated something, when they promised something; when the things promised come, the promises are taken away.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “A heart contrite and humbled God despiseth not.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Ye know that God is high: if thou shalt have made thyself high, He will be from thee; if thou shalt have humbled thyself, He will draw near to thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: See who this is: David as one man was seeming to implore; see ye here our image and the type of the Church.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Deal kindly, O Lord, in Thy good will with Sion” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: With this Sion deal kindly.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is Sion?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: A city holy.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is a city holy?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That which cannot be hidden, being upon a mountain established.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Sion in prospect, because it hath prospect of something which it hopeth for.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For Sion is interpreted “prospect,” and Jerusalem, “vision of peace.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Ye perceive then yourselves to be in Sion and in Jerusalem, if being sure ye look for hope that is to be, and if ye have peace with God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And be the walls of Jerusalem builded.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Deal kindly, O Lord, in Thy good will with Sion, and be the walls of Jerusalem builded.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For not to herself let Sion ascribe her merits: do Thou with her deal kindly, “Be the walls of Jerusalem builded:” be the battlements of our immortality laid, in faith and hope and charity.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Then Thou shalt accept the sacrifice of righteousness” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But now sacrifice for iniquity, to wit, a spirit troubled, and a heart humbled; then the sacrifice of righteousness, praises alone.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For, “Blessed they that dwell in Thy house, for ever and ever they shall praise Thee:” for this is the sacrifice of righteousness.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Oblations and holocausts.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What are “holocausts”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: A whole victim by fire consumed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When a whole beast was laid upon the altar with fire to be consumed, it was called a holocaust.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: May divine fire take us up whole, and that fervour catch us whole.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What fervour?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Neither is there that hideth himself from the heat thereof.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What fervour?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That whereof speaketh the Apostle: “In spirit fervent.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Be not merely our soul taken up by that divine fire of wisdom, but also our body; that it may earn their immortality; so be it lifted up for a holocaust, that death be swallowed into victory.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Oblations and holocausts.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Then shall they lay upon thine altar calves.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whence “calves”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What shall He therein choose?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Will it be the innocence of the new age, or necks freed from the yoke of the law?…
